headline: Cocoa Beach 2'+ clean lines but sandbars are shot. Longboard lines falling into troughs.
text: 1 PM lowtide update from Cocoa Beach's best surf spot — 2 foot plus with light winds and clean lines, but our sandbars are shot. A solid longboard line would hit the outside sandbar before immediately falling into a trough. The inside was closing out on lowtide. Water is warm though, so there's that.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Wednesday April 29, 2026 PM | 2ft+ Clean Lines, Poor Sandbars
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Wednesday April 29 afternoon: 2ft+ clean lines with light winds at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Sandbars in poor shape, longboard lines falling into troughs. Inside closing out at lowtide. Warm water.

headline: 2-3' breaking nearshore with ESE onshore bump. Offshore winds forecast but we're skeptical.
text: Morning update from Cocoa Beach's best surf spot — 2-3 foot breaking nearshore with high tide and ESE onshore winds putting a bump on it. Wind is forecast to turn offshore at some point this morning, but we remain skeptical. There's a rideable bump out there and it's a nice day if you need to get some waves.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Wednesday April 29, 2026 | 2-3ft Nearshore, ESE Onshore
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Wednesday April 29 morning: 2-3ft breaking nearshore at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. High tide with ESE onshore winds. Offshore wind shift forecast but uncertain. Rideable conditions.

headline: 2-3'+ bumpy but decent shortboard sets sneaking through. Bean Reef Surf Club made it work!
text: Afternoon high tide incoming check at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot — 2-3 foot plus and bumpy with ENE winds still on it, but occasional decent shortboard sets coming through. The Bean Reef Surf Club stopped by and made the most of the conditions. Swell is building and better winds are in the forecast for tomorrow and Thursday. Stay tuned.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Tuesday April 28, 2026 PM | 2-3ft+ Bumpy, Shortboard Sets
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Tuesday April 28 afternoon: 2-3ft+ bumpy surf with ENE winds at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Occasional shortboard sets. Bean Reef Surf Club session. Swell building for Wednesday.
keywords: Bean Reef Surf Club Cocoa Beach

headline: 2'+ choppy but workable for a longboard. Larger swell filling in tonight!
text: 8:15 AM outgoing tide check at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot — 2 foot plus breaking nearshore with ENE onshore winds chopping it up. Workable for a longboard if you're itching to paddle out. The good news: reports are showing a larger period swell filling in this evening with better winds coming tomorrow and Thursday. Could be a fun midweek run. Stay tuned.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Tuesday April 28, 2026 | 2ft+ Choppy, Swell Building Tonight
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Tuesday April 28: 2ft+ breaking nearshore with ENE onshore chop at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Longboard workable. Larger period swell filling in tonight with better winds Wednesday and Thursday.

headline: 2-3' choppy NE onshore — barely enough to make a board go. Tomorrow may be surfable.
text: 5:25 PM high tide check at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot — 2-3 foot and choppy with NE onshore winds making it bumpy and barely enough to push a board. Not the session you're looking for today. Good news is tomorrow's forecast is looking more surfable so we'll be back at first light to check. Stay tuned.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Monday April 27, 2026 PM | 2-3ft Choppy NE Onshore
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Monday April 27 afternoon: 2-3ft choppy surf with NE onshore winds at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Bumpy conditions, barely surfable. Better surf expected Tuesday.

headline: Cocoa Beach totally flat — 1' breaking into shore. Skimboard at high tide!
text: 7 AM high tide check at 31st Street — totally flat with 1 foot breaking into shore. Maybe something for a skimboard at high tide report time but that's about it. Looks like a little bump in the swell later this week. Stay tuned.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Monday April 27, 2026 | Flat 1ft, High Tide Skimboard
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Monday April 27: flat 1ft breaking into shore at Picnic Tables, 31st Street South Cocoa Beach. Skimboard conditions at high tide. Swell bump coming later this week.

headline: 1' nearshore with a SSW bump on the tiny line. Beautiful beach day!
text: Middle tide report from 31st Street — 1 foot breaking nearshore with SSW winds putting a bump on the tiny line. Not much to surf but an absolutely beautiful beach day at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot. Grab a towel and enjoy it.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Sunday April 26, 2026 | 1ft Nearshore, SSW Wind
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Sunday April 26: 1ft breaking nearshore at Picnic Tables, 31st Street. SSW winds, middle tide, beautiful beach day at South Cocoa Beach.

headline: Cocoa Beach 1' and tiny. Great day to learn or enjoy the beach!
text: Very small breaking against shore at 7 am. Maybe a bump for a very large board, but better day to teach the kids or enjoy the beautiful beach weather at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot!
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Saturday April 25, 2026 | 1ft Tiny, Beach Day
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Saturday April 25: 1ft tiny surf at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Great day to learn to surf or enjoy the beach.

headline: Cocoa Beach 2-3' and fun for a longboard. Winds finally down.
text: Lowtide report time, wind has come down considerably but so has the surf. It's 2-3' and probably fun for a longboard. Beautiful sunrise this morning at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot!
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Thursday April 23, 2026 | 2-3ft Longboard Fun
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Thursday April 23: 2-3ft fun longboard waves with light winds at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Beautiful sunrise.

headline: Cocoa Beach 2-3'+ with easing winds but still poor form at lowtide.
text: Wind has finally eased up some, but is still onshore with gusts over 12 kts. Surf has come down some as well 2-3'+ with still poor form. Lowtide at report time wasn't too appealing. Cocoa Beach surf conditions remain below average.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Wednesday April 22, 2026 | 2-3ft+ Easing Winds
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Wednesday April 22: 2-3ft+ with easing winds but poor form at lowtide. Onshore gusts over 12kts at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach.

headline: Cocoa Beach 3-5' washing machine continues. ENE winds keeping quality low.
text: Choppy washing machine conditions continue with erosion visible on the beach. 3-5' at lowtide but persistent ENE winds have the quality low across Cocoa Beach.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Tuesday April 21, 2026 PM | 3-5ft Choppy Washing Machine
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Tuesday April 21 evening: 3-5ft choppy washing machine conditions with ENE winds at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Beach erosion visible.

headline: Cocoa Beach 3-5' but a choppy, wind-blown mess. Stay home and walk the dog.
text: Extremely windy, low tide incoming and a chopped up mess at Cocoa Beach. Surfable, but doesn't look like much fun. Stay home and walk the dog.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Tuesday April 21, 2026 | 3-5ft Windy Choppy Mess
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Tuesday April 21: 3-5ft but extremely windy and choppy at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Low tide incoming, poor conditions.

headline: 2-3'+ wind driven victory at sea conditions.
text: A fast drift and maybe a surfable wave at high tide incoming at report time. Feels like a Monday, happy high holiday to those who celebrate. Cocoa Beach's best surf spot doing its thing.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Monday April 20, 2026 AM | 2-3ft+ Wind Swell
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Monday April 20 morning: 2-3ft+ wind driven surf at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. High tide incoming, fast drift conditions.
